name: Raw Cacao Superfood Truffles
intro: Most raw date chocolate truffle recipes, while delicious, taste very similar. That is, they taste more like date confections to me than chocolate. In my recipe, the chocolate taste is pronounced, and the combination of the superfoods lucuma and maca, plus a small amount of coconut sugar, results in a more complex flavor. For a “raw friendly” not “raw really” truffle, use lightly toasted pecans (page 19).

ingredients.item: 4.5 ounces / 126 grams pitted Medjool dates (about 8 large or ½ cup packed)
/ 15 grams raw cacao powder, plus more for coating (optional)
/ 27 grams coconut sugar, plus more for rolling (optional)

method: Chop the pecans in a food processor by pulsing the machine a few times, then process to a fine meal. Do not process so long that the nuts become butter.

Add the dates, cacao powder, lucuma, maca, coconut sugar, and vanilla extract and process until the mixture looks like fine crumbs. The mixture will not be a paste, but it will hold together when pressed between your fingers.
Spoon the dough into a small bowl. Scoop out 1-tablespoon portions and press between your fingers until the dough holds together. Roll into rounds in the palms of your hands.
Roll the truffles in the extra coconut sugar or keep them plain.

Refrigerate for 1 hour to allow the flavors to develop before serving. Enjoy these truffles at room temperature or chilled. The truffles will stay fresh for a few weeks, but I like the flavor best when eaten within one week. Freeze in an airtight container for up to one month.
